summary: User reports a NullReferenceException in SKXamlCanvas.Skia.cs when the canvas size changes. The issue is suspected to be caused by FreeBitmap resetting the bitmap field to null.

analysis.summary: The `SKXamlCanvas.Skia.cs` implementation of `CreateBitmap` has a logic flaw where `FreeBitmap()` can be called twice. The second call (triggered when pixel buffer needs resizing) nulls out the `bitmap` field, but the code fails to recreate the `bitmap` before returning. This leaves `bitmap` as null when `DoInvalidate` subsequently tries to access `bitmap.PixelBuffer`, causing a NullReferenceException.

codeInvestigation.finding: In `CreateBitmap`, if `pixels` array needs reallocation (e.g. size change), `FreeBitmap()` is called. `FreeBitmap` sets `bitmap` to null. There is no code path to recreate `bitmap` after this second call before the method returns.

keySignals.text: This line of code may throw NRE if the canvas changed its size, because the FreeBitmap method may have reset bitmap field to null.

analysis.rationale: Clear logic error in resource management within `CreateBitmap` method. Code inspection confirms the path where `bitmap` is nulled and not re-initialized.

proposals.title: Recreate bitmap after second FreeBitmap call
proposals.description: Add a check after the second `FreeBitmap` call to ensure `bitmap` is recreated if it is null and dimensions are valid.

resolution.recommendedProposal: Recreate bitmap after second FreeBitmap call
resolution.recommendedReason: Directly addresses the missing initialization path.

actions.description: Confirm the bug via code inspection and explain the NRE cause.
actions.comment: Confirmed the issue via code inspection. The `CreateBitmap` method calls `FreeBitmap` when the pixel buffer needs resizing, which nulls out the `bitmap`. However, it fails to recreate the `bitmap` before returning, leading to the NRE in `DoInvalidate`.
